generated_for: Bothria Silk Lab - procedural spider locomotion (Three.js / glTF)

units.note: 1 Blender unit = 1 meter as authored, but the model is at game scale (see dimensions). Runtime may scale SpiderRoot freely; all bones are scale 1,1,1.

coordinate_conventions.description: One consistent convention across the whole armature. Values given in BOTH the Blender authoring space (Z-up) and the exported glTF space (Y-up). The glTF exporter maps (x,y,z)_blender -> (x, z, -y)_gltf.

gltf_threejs_space.note: In Three.js the model faces -X with +Y up. This is NOT the Three.js default of -Z forward; read 'forward' from this file rather than assuming. To make the model face -Z instead, apply a -90 deg rotation about +Y to SpiderRoot (or the loaded scene) at runtime.

bone_local_axes.primary_axis: bone local +Y points from head to tail (along the limb segment)
bone_local_axes.dorsal_axis: bone local +Z aligned toward world/dorsal up (roll normalized via align-to-global-Z)
bone_local_axes.bend_axis: bone local X is the primary hinge/bend axis for leg joints
bone_local_axes.mirror: Left and right corresponding bones have mirror-symmetric local frames; applying an equal local rotation to a L/R pair produces mirrored motion.

reference_bones.HeadReference: forward-facing reference at the front tip of the head (non-deform)
reference_bones.SupportReference: preferred central support position beneath the thorax (non-deform)

spinnerets.Spinneret_Center: central silk emission point at posterior-ventral abdomen tip
spinnerets.note: Non-deform bones. Query their world positions at runtime to originate silk.

fangs.note: Chelicerae/fangs. Optional and cosmetic. Asymmetric in the source model (left=3 bones, right=1).

reach_units.note: Distance from Coxa head to FootTip head, in model units. 'max' = fully extended (sum of segment lengths). 'comfortable' = rest-pose distance. 'min' = approximate fully-compressed reach (~0.35 x max, validate in engine).

neutral_pose.description: The armature rest pose IS the neutral procedural stance. No separate animation clip is required.
neutral_pose.note: No leg is straight (all knee angles < 152 deg), so procedural IK has an unambiguous preferred bend direction.

recommended_joint_limits_deg.note: APPROXIMATE, biologically plausible ranges for the runtime to reject impossible footholds. NOT enforced by Blender constraints (none survive glTF). Angles are local-space relative to the bone axes above: bend = rotation about local X (primary hinge), swing = about local Z, twist = about local Y (keep near zero). Validate/tune in engine.

notes_asymmetry_and_limitations: Legs L4 and R4 (rear) carry an extra Tarsus segment (6 deform bones); legs 1-3 have 5. Every leg has Coxa, Femur, Patella, Tibia, Metatarsus and a FootTip; the two rear legs additionally have Tarsus. FootTip is present and correctly placed at the distal silk-contact point on all 8 legs.
Chelicerae/fangs are asymmetric in the source: Fang_L has 3 bones, Fang_R has 1. Cosmetic only.

The source mesh is very slightly asymmetric left/right (< ~0.03 units, < ~1 deg). Bone rolls were normalized (local Z toward global up), giving mirror-symmetric L/R frames to within that residual source asymmetry (max axis deviation ~0.85 deg).
A fur particle system (hair) exists in the .blend but is intentionally excluded from the GLB (glTF has no hair representation). Fur can be re-created procedurally in Three.js if desired.
No Blender IK / Follow-Path / driver / custom-property behavior is exported; the skeleton is pure FK and must be driven by the Three.js locomotion system.
FootHome_* bones are static neutral-stance references parented to Thorax; they are NOT live IK targets. Runtime creates its own dynamic foot-contact targets.
